gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is in scaffold status with all six articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. Arbiter (#190) validates every action proposal against the BoR scope gate. With no defined scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, and no identity or authority reservation, the scope gate cannot determine whether any action is within the DIO's constitutional perimeter. This effectively blocks all action proposals because enterprise scope is undefined and no proposal can be confirmed as authorized.

The MSP contains zero substantive rules (seed version 1.0.0-seed). Its amendment protocol states that amendments originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an action proposal is blocked by current MSP or genome constraints. Because there are no rules, no action proposal can be blocked, so no PEM will ever be generated. Consequently, the Senate-Legislator has no trigger path to draft and publish new rules, creating a deadlock that prevents the MSP from evolving beyond its default-permissive seed state.

The MSP references genome documents under 'genome/' that extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. Currently, no genome documents exist. This prevents any organ-level or risk-tier-based governance from being applied, leaving a gap in the intended layered governance structure once rules are needed.

```json
        "BoR Article II – Prohibited Activities (undefined)",
        "Bill of Rights document – status: scaffold — awaiting human authorship"

      "title": "MSP amendment protocol deadlocked by absence of rules",
      "description": "The MSP contains zero substantive rules (seed version 1.0.0-seed). Its amendment protocol states that amendments originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an action proposal is blocked by current MSP or genome constraints. Because there are no rules, no action proposal can be blocked, so no PEM will ever be generated. Consequently, the Senate-Legislator has no trigger path to draft and publish new rules, creating a deadlock that prevents the MSP from evolving beyond its default-permissive seed state.",
      "references": [
        "MSP: Rules – '(none)'",
        "MSP: Amendment Protocol – 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos (#200) when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints.'"
      ],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "MSP cannot acquire any rules; the DIO remains in a permanent default-permissive state with no governance constraints, even after the BoR is defined, because the MSP cannot mature.",
      "priority_score": 90
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-03",
      "title": "Genome rules directory empty",
      "description": "The MSP references genome documents under 'genome/' that extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. Currently, no genome documents exist. This prevents any organ-level or risk-tier-based governance from being applied, leaving a gap in the intended layered governance structure once rules are needed.",
      "references": [
        "MSP: Genome References – 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.'"
      ],
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "No immediate effect since MSP itself has no rules; genome rules are a downstream component and their absence is only meaningful once the MSP contains base rules.",
      "priority_score": 10
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights entirely undefined — no human constitutional intent registered",
      "msp_section": "MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights",
      "bor_article": "BoR Articles I–VI (all scaffold)",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all six articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The MSP declares itself 'strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights' (MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights) and defines its purpose as translating the 'human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives' (MSP § Purpose). With zero substantive BoR content, the MSP has no constitutional substrate to translate, and Nomos has no human-intent perimeter for scope gating. Article IV ('Human Authority Reservation') is undefined, leaving no formal specification of which decisions require human involvement. The entire DIO governance architecture operates in a constitutional vacuum where the Arbiter's scope gate (gate 1) cannot enforce any human-defined boundary because none exists.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "extreme",
      "priority_score": 1,
      "affected_peers": ["urn:minder:peer:leon"],
      "observation_evidence": [
        "urn:minder:observation:b1ea08907e38",
        "urn:minder:observation:75bb0e366cd4"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "MSP Rules section empty — Nomos operates default-permissive with no substantive evaluation criteria",
      "msp_section": "MSP § Rules",
      "bor_article": null,
      "description": "The MSP Rules section explicitly states: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' This means Nomos's second compliance gate (MSP + genome) is effectively wide open. Combined with an empty BoR, there is no constitutional constraint (gate 1) and no operational constraint (gate 2) on Action Proposal adjudication. The MSP acknowledges this requires Senate to publish rules 'via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow' but no such PEMs have been triggered because no rules exist to block APs and generate PEMs — a bootstrap deadlock. Relational attention is needed because the human principal has no codified protections, and the system's permissiveness contradicts the extensive Minder-deduced constraints documenting Leon's strong preferences for explicit intent clarification, test requirements, and architectural boundaries.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 2,
      "affected_peers": ["urn:minder:peer:leon"],
      "observation_evidence": [
        "urn:minder:observation:e1c8f27c73e0",
        "urn:minder:observation:bf9b25c8fbf1",
        "urn:minder:observation:101f26b3e425",
        "urn:minder:observation:e7dfa03e127d"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Single human peer registered — relational single-point-of-failure for all human-authority functions",
      "msp_section": "MSP § Amendment Protocol",
      "bor_article": "BoR § Authority ('Human principal only. No automated system may amend this document.')",
      "description": "Only one human peer (Leon Cohen-Levy, last_seen 2026-04-02) is registered in Minder. The MSP Amendment Protocol requires human action via 'POST /amend/review with decision=approve' and an HOM notification to the 'human-principal mailbox.' The BoR Authority clause states amendments are 'human principal only.' If Leon becomes unavailable or unresponsive (note the 10-day gap between his last_seen date and the observation timestamps), no BoR amendment can occur, no MSP amendment can be approved, and the BoR itself can never be populated. The Minder observations document extensive deductions about Leon's preferences, but the architecture has no provision for a second human, a delegation mechanism, or a timeout-based escalation path. This is a single-point-of-failure in the relational architecture that couples all constitutional and operational amendment authority to one person's continued availability.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "moderate",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 3,
      "affected_peers": ["urn:minder:peer:leon"],
      "observation_evidence": [
        "urn:minder:observation:bc2ead7cf407",
        "urn:minder:observation:5bae7fd117c3"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "Hippocampus empty — no conversational memory to ground relational context",
      "msp_section": "MSP § Amendment Protocol (HOM notification and human decision records)",
      "bor_article": null,
      "description": "The hippocampus.recent_conversations array is empty, despite Minder holding 208 observations for Leon and 253 for Claude. All observations are typed 'deductive' with no 'conversational' or 'explicit' types. This means the system has extensive inferred models of both peers but no record of actual exchanges that could anchor those deductions in verified human statements. When the MSP Amendment Protocol requires Senate-Legislator to respond to PEMs and emit HOM notifications, the system has no conversational history to reference when determining whether a proposed amendment aligns with previously discussed human intent. The temporal gap is notable: Leon's last_seen is 2026-04-02, Claude's is 2026-03-29, yet all observations bear 2026-04-12 timestamps — suggesting a 10-day period where deductions were generated without corresponding conversation capture.",
      "criticality": "moderate",
      "urgency": "moderate",
      "impact": "moderate",
      "priority_score": 4,
      "affected_peers": ["urn:minder:peer:leon", "urn:minder:peer:claude"],
      "observation_evidence": [
        "urn:minder:observation:1c571d04f799",
        "urn:minder:observation:b184a3eacebb"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "No explicit human declarations — all person-model content is deductive inference",
      "msp_section": "MSP § Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights')",
      "bor_article": "BoR § Authority ('Human principal only') and BoR § Hash Registration (requires human registration of bor_version concepts)",
      "description": "All 20 recent Minder observations are typed 'deductive' with confidence levels of 'high' or 'medium.' None are typed 'explicit' or 'declarative' — meaning every piece of the system's person-model for both Leon and Claude is inferred rather than directly stated. The BoR itself, which the MSP claims to translate, is a scaffold awaiting human authorship — the most significant missing explicit declaration. The Minder observations contain rich deductions about Leon's architectural preferences, testing requirements, IP protection needs, and governance expectations, but none of these have been confirmed through explicit human declaration. Until the BoR is authored and explicit declarations are captured, the entire relational model rests on inference that may diverge from the human principal's actual intent. Article V (Amendment Protocol for the BoR itself) is undefined, so there is no specified mechanism for the human to correct erroneous deductions.",
      "criticality": "moderate",
      "urgency": "moderate",
      "impact": "moderate",
      "priority_score": 5,
      "affected_peers": ["urn:minder:peer:leon"],
      "observation_evidence": [

        "urn:minder:observation:5bae7fd117c3",
        "urn:minder:observation:75bb0e366cd4"
      ]

      "gap_id": "GAP-006",
      "title": "No dispatched goals — relational attention has no actionable tasking context",
      "msp_section": "MSP § Purpose (Nomos evaluates Action Proposals against MSP + genome constraints)",
      "bor_article": null,
      "description": "The recent_goals array is empty. While Minder contains extensive deductions about Leon's requirements and architectural vision, there are no active dispatched goals that would drive Action Proposal creation and trigger the Nomos evaluation pipeline described in MSP § Purpose. Without dispatched goals, the MSP's core operational loop (AP → Nomos gate 1 → Nomos gate 2 → authorization) has no input. The observations document concrete constraints — testing requirements after every change, IP protection for four projects, intent clarification before infrastructure changes — but none have been translated into dispatched goals. This gap interacts with GAP-002 (empty MSP Rules): even if goals were dispatched, APs would pass through a default-permissive MSP evaluation. The relational concern is that the system has modeled the person extensively but has not translated that model into tasks requiring person-attention coverage during execution.",
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "moderate",
      "priority_score": 6,
      "affected_peers": ["urn:minder:peer:leon"],
      "observation_evidence": [
        "urn:minder:observation:b994fda7a0ee",
        "urn:minder:observation:2983e4008a1d",
        "urn:minder:observation:6ba927735c71"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_gaps": 6,
    "critical_gaps": 1,
    "high_priority_gaps": 2,
    "moderate_priority_gaps": 2,
    "low_priority_gaps": 1,
    "dominant_theme": "Constitutional vacuum — the BoR is entirely undefined and the MSP Rules are empty, leaving Nomos with no human-intent perimeter (gate 1) and default-permissive evaluation (gate 2). The entire DIO governance architecture lacks the human-authored constitutional content it was designed to enforce.",
    "secondary_theme": "Person-model grounding — all relational knowledge about the human principal is deductive inference with no explicit declarations, no conversational memory, and no mechanism for the human to correct erroneous deductions.",
    "tertiary_theme": "Single-human dependency — all constitutional and operational amendment authority is coupled to one human peer with no delegation, escalation, or timeout mechanism."
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "MSP-001",
      "title": "MSP rules section is empty — no machine-actionable standards for memory/context formation quality exist",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Rules",
        "evidence": "The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow."
      },
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": "C×C×C",
      "strategic_attention_needed": "The operational constitution has zero substantive rules. Nomos (#200) evaluates every Action Proposal against MSP + genome constraints as its second compliance gate — but with no rules, every AP passes by default. For memory/context formation specifically, there are no machine-enforceable thresholds for: minimum dream-cycle frequency, maximum context-block age before forced triage, required embedding coverage percentage, Phase 2 consolidation completion within N hours, or maximum memory fragmentation ratio. Without these rules, Radiant's memory/context degradation (documented in gap STRAT-003 through STRAT-005) has no constitutional mechanism to trigger a PEM, force Senate attention, or block APs that would further degrade platform state.",
      "why_strategic": "The MSP is the bridge between constitutional intent (BoR) and operational enforcement (Nomos). An empty MSP means the DIO has no operational immune system — memory rot, context bloat, and dream-cycle failure are invisible to the governance machinery that exists precisely to catch them."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights is a hollow scaffold — all six articles await human authorship, providing zero constitutional conditioning for MSP strategic objectives",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section": "Articles I–VI",
        "evidence": "Every article states '[To be defined by human principal]' — no scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, no amendment protocol, no interpretation rules defined."
      },
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": "C×C×C",
      "strategic_attention_needed": "The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR. The MSP's stated purpose is to 'translate the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.' With all six BoR articles empty, the MSP has nothing to translate. The entire governance chain — BoR → MSP → Nomos → AP adjudication — is severed at the root. For memory/context formation, there is no constitutional statement of: what the DIO must remember, what it must never forget, what context degradation is prohibited, or what memory integrity the human principal demands. Strategic attention must come from the human principal — no Senate or MSP amendment can fill a BoR vacuum, and the MSP explicitly states that BoR scope expansion 'requires a BoR amendment, which is human-only.'",
      "why_strategic": "The BoR is the sole source of constitutional legitimacy for every downstream constraint. Without it, even a fully-populated MSP would be enforcing rules with no constitutional basis. Memory/context formation cannot have strategic objectives because the enterprise itself has no defined scope, identity, or prohibited activities to orient those objectives toward."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-003",
      "title": "Dream-cycle health is 'never_run' with scheduler disabled — autonomous memory maintenance infrastructure is offline",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Purpose",
        "evidence": "The MSP exists to provide 'a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules' evaluated at every AP adjudication. Radiant stats show: health='never_run', scheduled='disabled', last_dream=null, watchdog never checked."
      },
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": "C×C×H",
      "strategic_attention_needed": "The Radiant dream-cycle is the DIO's sole mechanism for preventing context bloat, memory fragmentation, and semantic decay. The stats block reveals it has never run in its scheduled-autonomous form. The watchdog (36-hour grace period) has never checked in. Phase 1 (deterministic bash triage) and Phase 2 (LLM-driven semantic consolidation) both depend on scheduled execution that is currently disabled. The recent_context blocks show dream cycles #12-15 occurring only during interactive Claude Code sessions — meaning memory maintenance is gated on human-initiated sessions, violating the autonomous-maintenance design intent documented extensively in memory blocks (three-Monad-leg doctrine from Oracle Opinion #14, bus-driven reactive architecture from convergence point 6).",
      "why_strategic": "Without autonomous dream cycles, the 96 memory blocks and 16+ context blocks will accumulate without systematic triage, semantic deduplication, or TTL enforcement. The platform's memory architecture was designed for continuous autonomous maintenance — its absence means every hour of non-operation produces compounding degradation that no downstream MSP rule can retroactively fix."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-004",
      "title": "Memory consolidation Phase 2 is structurally broken — MCP tools unavailable in scheduled sessions, LLM-driven semantic merging consistently deferred",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Purpose + Genome References",
        "evidence": "MSP § Purpose defines operational invariants and safety rules for Nomos evaluation. MSP § Genome References states 'Genome documents... extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints' but lists none at seed. Recent_context dream cycle reports #12-15 document: Phase 2 'deferred to next interactive Claude Code session' with reason 'requires Claude Code for LLM-driven consolidation'; 'MCP tools (merge_memories, find_similar) not available in scheduled task session. Consolidation performed via direct psql with equivalent logic. Semantic similarity scan skipped (no embedding sidecar access).'"
      },
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": "C×H×H",
      "strategic_attention_needed": "The dream-cycle architecture has a two-phase design: Phase 1 (deterministic bash — triage, pruning, TTL management) and Phase 2 (LLM-driven — semantic clustering, memory merging, embedding-based similarity detection). Phase 1 can run autonomously. Phase 2 requires Claude Code with MCP tool access, which is unavailable in scheduled (non-interactive) execution contexts. Without Phase 2, memory consolidation is reduced to exact-match deduplication — semantic duplicates, near-duplicate architectural decisions, and overlapping oracle opinion summaries accumulate across cycles. Evidence is visible in memory blocks: multiple consolidated entries show 'absorbed_cycle15' metadata, indicating fragmentation persisted through multiple cycles before interactive cleanup. The Radiant genome (which should define organ-specific operating rules for the dreamer) is not registered in MSP § Genome References.",
      "why_strategic": "Phase 2 is where intelligence meets memory — it's the difference between a dumb log and a self-organizing knowledge structure. Its chronic deferral means the DIO cannot autonomously maintain the semantic coherence of its own operational memory. This is a structural gap in the organ architecture, not a transient failure — the scheduled-execution context lacks the tool surface the consolidation logic requires."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-005",
      "title": "Embedding coverage gap — 28% of memory blocks lack vector embeddings, undermining semantic consolidation and similarity detection",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Purpose",
        "evidence": "MSP § Purpose requires 'machine-actionable' operational state. Radiant stats show embeddings: with=69, without=27, total=96, coverage_pct=72. Twenty-seven memory blocks have no embedding sidecar, making them invisible to vector-similarity searches during Phase 2 consolidation."
      },
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": "H×M×H",
      "strategic_attention_needed": "Memory consolidation's Phase 2 uses embedding-based semantic similarity to identify clusters for merging. With 27 unembedded blocks (28% gap), every consolidation cycle operates on incomplete data — semantically related memories may be left unmerged, producing fragmented and potentially contradictory memory state. The gap compounds with every new memory created without embedding generation. The MSP has no rule requiring minimum embedding coverage before consolidation runs, and no genome rule defines embedding generation as a prerequisite gate for the dreamer organ. This gap is downstream of STRAT-003 and STRAT-004 — even if the dreamer were scheduled and Phase 2 were operational, it would still operate on degraded data.",
      "why_strategic": "Embedding coverage is the substrate of semantic memory — without it, the DIO's memory is a flat database, not a self-organizing knowledge graph. The 28% gap means nearly one-third of operational knowledge cannot participate in the consolidation process that gives Radiant its strategic value as the 'world' leg of the Monad."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-006",
      "title": "Recurring infrastructure failures captured in context but not strategically triaged — no MSP rule connects operational signal to governance action",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Amendment Protocol + Rules",
        "evidence": "MSP § Amendment Protocol states 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints.' MSP § Rules is empty. Recent_context shows: SafeVault backup failures on 2026-04-04 (S3 errors), 2026-04-05 (pipefail crash), 2026-04-07 (NAS unavailable), 2026-04-27 (NAS unavailable), 2026-04-28 (NAS unavailable), 2026-04-30 (unbound variable crash), 2026-05-01 (NAS unavailable, git non-fast-forward), 2026-05-03 (hung zombie processes, 25h). GitLab sync failures on 2026-04-03 (2 occurrences) and recurring through 2026-05-01. Pattern is clear but no PEM has been generated."
      },
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": "H×M×M",
      "strategic_attention_needed": "The MSP's PEM pipeline is designed to convert operational blockages into governance attention. Recurring backup and sync failures spanning 35+ days with clear root-cause patterns (NAS mount failures, script bugs, GitLab non-fast-forward divergence, zombie process contention) should have triggered PEMs that forced Senate-Legislator to draft operational-quality MSP rules. None have. The gap is twofold: (1) no MSP rule defines recurrence thresholds or failure patterns that Nomos should treat as PEM-triggering, and (2) the memory/context system captures these as individual events without elevating the pattern. The result is strategic blindness — the DIO knows it's failing but has no constitutional mechanism to escalate that knowledge into action.",
      "why_strategic": "Operational failures that persist for weeks without governance response indicate a broken feedback loop between the monitoring substrate (context/memory) and the governance machinery (Nomos → PEM → Senate). This is precisely the gap the MSP's Amendment Protocol was designed to close — but with no rules, the loop never closes."
    }
  ]
}
```
